tier_1_structural.name: Structural
tier_1_structural.unlock_rule: SI1 (P1) must be mastered before Core unlocks

SI1.name: Instructional Clarity
SI1.rationale: Without a clear goal, students cannot orientate to the lesson. Everything else depends on this.

SI2.name: Logical Flow
SI2.rationale: A lesson without clear structure loses students mid-way. Foundational but recoverable if slightly weak.

SI3.name: Subject Content Accuracy
SI3.rationale: Inaccurate content causes lasting harm to student understanding. Non-negotiable regardless of everything else.

tier_2_core.name: Core
tier_2_core.unlock_rule: PIC-1 (P1) mastered + at least 3 of remaining Core indicators consistently strong

PIC-1.name: Activities & Tasks Alignment
PIC-1.rationale: If tasks don't match the learning objective or cognitive level, the lesson is structurally broken regardless of delivery quality.

PIC-3.name: Understanding Student Misconceptions
PIC-3.rationale: Unaddressed misconceptions compound over time. Catching and correcting them is high-leverage teaching.

PIC-4.name: Quality Questioning
PIC-4.rationale: Open-ended and probing questions are the primary vehicle for moving students beyond recall into reasoning.

PIC-2.name: Use of Academic Language
PIC-2.rationale: Subject-specific vocabulary is essential for conceptual development. Weak modelling limits how students think and express ideas.

PIC-5.name: Effective Feedback
PIC-5.rationale: Specific verbal feedback accelerates learning but lessons can function without it being consistently strong.

tier_3_advanced.name: Advanced
tier_3_advanced.unlock_rule: PIA-3 (P1) mastered + at least 3 of remaining Advanced indicators consistently strong

PIA-3.name: Catering to Learning Levels
PIA-3.rationale: Without verbal differentiation, struggling students fall further behind and advanced students disengage. Highest equity impact in this tier.

PIA-4.name: Responsive Re-explanation
PIA-4.rationale: When confusion is met with repetition rather than a new approach, learning stalls. High-leverage instructional skill.

PIA-1.name: Prior Knowledge Activation
PIA-1.rationale: Activating prior knowledge bridges what students know to what they are learning. High impact when done well.

MA-0.name: Instructional Model Structure (GRR)
MA-0.rationale: Gradual Release of Responsibility ensures students progressively move toward independence. Weak GRR keeps students dependent on the teacher.

PIA-2.name: Meaningful Connections
PIA-2.rationale: Real-life connections and analogies make abstract content accessible. Strong lever for comprehension and retention.

PIA-5.name: Student Agency & Participation
PIA-5.rationale: Important for ownership and engagement but requires strong foundational and core skills to be sustainably implemented.

tier_4_subject_specific.name: Subject-Specific
tier_4_subject_specific.unlock_rule: PIA-3 (P1) mastered + at least 3 of remaining Advanced indicators consistently strong

M1.name: Mathematical Discourse & Reasoning
M1.rationale: Justifying solutions is the core of mathematical thinking. Without it students are computing, not reasoning.
M1.subject: mathematics

M2.name: Problem-Solving & Productive Struggle
M2.rationale: Think time and tolerance for struggle are validated predictors of deep mathematical understanding.
M2.subject: mathematics

L1.name: Explicit Phonics / Decoding
L1.rationale: Foundational literacy skill. Weak phonics instruction has direct and lasting impact on reading ability.
L1.subject: literacy

L2.name: Comprehension Strategy Instruction
L2.rationale: Metacognitive reading strategies bridge decoding and deep comprehension.
L2.subject: literacy

L3.name: Reading-Writing Connections
L3.rationale: Valuable integration skill but requires strong decoding and comprehension foundations first.
L3.subject: literacy

S1.name: Inquiry-Based Approach
S1.rationale: Inquiry before explanation is the defining feature of science pedagogy. Its absence reduces science to fact transmission.
S1.subject: science

S2.name: Science Talk & Student Sense-Making
S2.rationale: Students verbalising science understanding is the primary signal that learning is happening, not just listening.
S2.subject: science

prioritySelectionRules.rule_1: Always flag P1 gaps first across all active tiers
prioritySelectionRules.rule_2: Fill remaining slots with P2 gaps — lower tier first (Structural → Core → Advanced → Subject-Specific)
prioritySelectionRules.rule_3: P3 gaps only surface when all P1 and P2 gaps in active tiers are resolved
prioritySelectionRules.rule_4: Subject-Specific indicators follow the same P1/P2/P3 logic — a subject P1 is treated identically to a universal P1 once Subject-Specific is unlocked
prioritySelectionRules.rule_5: Cap at 3 items total — hold the rest for the next cycle
prioritySelectionRules.rule_6: If multiple P2 gaps exist across tiers, lower tier takes precedence — Structural P2 before Core P2 before Advanced P2 before Subject-Specific P2

slgpTreatment.description: Student Learning Gain Proxies (SLGP-1 to SLGP-5)
slgpTreatment.visibility: Backend signals only - never appear in teacher-facing feedback
slgpTreatment.cap_impact: Never count toward the 3-item feedback cap

slgp_weak_indicator_flagged.effect: Feedback carries slightly more urgency — 'students are not yet showing this in their responses'

slgp_strong_indicator_flagged.effect: Feedback is gentler — 'students are responding well, this just needs more consistency'

slgp_weak_indicator_strong.effect: Coach dashboard flag only — warrants closer observation next cycle. Not surfaced to teacher.
